steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks why Rafael accompanies his brother. The answer must be grounded only in what the passage directly states — no inferences beyond the text are allowed.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Rafael has learned all he can from his brother and hopes that the sisters will be able to give him additional guidance.": it is the narrator who has learned all Alejandro could teach her, not Rafael. Rafael's motive is unrelated. ✗
  • "Rafael has not received formal instruction in poetry and wants to ask the sisters' famous father to be his mentor.": the passage says Rafael wanted to "make the acquaintance" of the poet — a social introduction, not a request for mentorship. ✗
  • "Rafael aspires to present his poems to the sisters and ask for their opinion on his writing.": nowhere does the passage mention Rafael planning to share his own writing. ✗
  • "Rafael anticipates having the opportunity to be introduced to both the sisters and their father.": the passage states Rafael was "hoping not only to meet us but to make the acquaintance of the poet himself" — exactly two goals, both captured here. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
